description: Lock urban sanitary surveys to the documented 1838 population-health and environmental inquiry method without later treatment or imaging dependencies.

checks.reason: Municipal chlorination followed the documented sanitary inquiries by decades and is a possible intervention, not an inquiry prerequisite.
The 1838 environmental and population-health survey workflow predates X-rays and does not require clinical imaging.
The primary inquiry record specifies disease returns, institutional data, local reports, and comparative factual analysis.
Organized health data enabled the surveys while personal inspection and descriptive evidence remained additional methods.
The 1842 Poor Law Commission report identifies metropolitan fever and sanitary-condition inquiries presented on 14 May 1838.
